Question: Classify each of the following equations as linear or nonlinear. If the equation is linear, determine further whether it is homogeneous or nonhomogeneous. $$( y^{\second} )^{2} - y^{\prime} + 8 x^{3} y = 0$$ $$( \text{sin} t ) y^{\second} + \text{cos} t - 3 t y^{\prime} = 0$$
Show that $$y = 2 x^{2}$$ is a solution to the differential equation $$\frac{1}{2} x^{2} y^{\second} - x y^{\prime} + y = 0 .$$

Consider the differential equation $$y^{\second} + 5 y^{\prime} + 6 y = 0 .$$ Given that $$e^{-2 x}$$ and $$e^{-3 x}$$ are solutions to this differential equation, show that $$3 e^{-2 x} + 6 e^{-3 x}$$ is a solution.
Determine whether the following pairs of functions are linearly dependent or linearly independent: $$f_{1} ( x ) = e^{x} ,$$ $$f_{2} ( x ) = 3 e^{3 x} .$$
If $$y_{1} ( x ) = e^{3 x}$$ and $$y_{2} ( x ) = x e^{3 x}$$ are solutions to $$y^{\second} - 6 y^{\prime} + 9 y = 0 ,$$ what is the general solution?
Find the general solution to the following differential equations: $$y^{\second} - 2 y^{\prime} + 10 y = 0$$ $$y^{\second} + 14 y^{\prime} + 49 y = 0$$
Solve the initial-value problem $$y^{\second} - 3 y^{\prime} - 10 y = 0 ,$$ $$y ( 0 ) = 0 ,$$ $$y^{\prime} ( 0 ) = 7 .$$
Solve the following initial-value problem and graph the solution: $$y^{\second} - 2 y^{\prime} + 10 y = 0 , y ( 0 ) = 2 , y^{\prime} ( 0 ) = -1$$

Suppose the following initial-value problem models the position (in feet) of a mass in a spring-mass system at any given time. Solve the initial-value problem and graph the solution. What is the position of the mass at time $$t = 0.3$$ sec? How fast is it moving at time $$t = 0.1$$ sec? In what direction? $$y^{\second} + 14 y^{\prime} + 49 y = 0 , y ( 0 ) = 0 , y^{\prime} ( 0 ) = 1$$
Answer: $$y ( t ) = t e^{-7 t}$$ At time $$t = 0.3 ,$$ $$y ( 0.3 ) = 0.3 e^{( -7 * 0.3 )} = 0.3 e^{-2.1} \approx 0.0367 .$$ The mass is 0.0367 ft below equilibrium. At time $$t = 0.1 ,$$ $$y^{\prime} ( 0.1 ) = 0.3 e^{-0.7} \approx 0.1490 .$$ The mass is moving downward at a speed of 0.1490 ft/sec.
